steps.title: Find the greatest common factor
steps.content:
In $7x^{6}-14x^{2}$, both terms share common factors:
  • Coefficients: 7 and 14 are both divisible by 7
  • Variables: $x^6$ and $x^2$ are both divisible by $x^2$
The GCF is $7x^2$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Dividing each term by $7x^2$:
$7x^6 ÷ 7x^2 = x^4$ and $14x^2 ÷ 7x^2 = 2$

So:
$$7x^{6}-14x^{2} = 7x^{2}(x^{4}-2)$$This matches choice D — D is correct.
